user.title: Connect to CANtera
user.description:
Enter the IP address or hostname of the Raspberry Pi running the CANtera OBD-II logger.

You can use the Pi's local network IP (e.g. `192.168.x.x`), a hostname, or a Tailscale IP (`100.x.x.x`) if Home Assistant is also connected to your Tailscale network.

Start CANtera with `cantera -b 500000 log-obd --api` to enable the HTTP API.

reconfigure.title: Reconfigure CANtera
reconfigure.description: Update the host or port for your CANtera device.

error.cannot_connect: Unable to connect to the CANtera API. Check that the Pi is reachable and running `cantera log-obd --api`.
error.host_unreachable: Host unreachable. Verify the IP address, that the Pi is powered on, and that it is reachable from Home Assistant (check firewall and network routing).
error.connection_refused: Connection refused. The host is reachable but CANtera is not listening on this port. Start it with `cantera log-obd --api`.
